item.title: UK economy failed to grow in January ahead of Iran war
item.description: Analysts had been expecting 0.2% growth for the UK economy at the beginning of the year.

item.title: Rescue operations under way after US refuelling plane crashes in Iraq
item.description: The US Central Command says rescue efforts are under way after a refuelling aircraft went down over western Iraq.

item.title: The Iranian school bombing and the information war
item.description: Evidence suggests that the US was most likely to be behind the attack that killed over 100 children

item.title: Iran war tests Xi’s plan to build China’s stockpiles
item.description: Beijing has driven major increase in strategic commodity inventories but their scale remains unclear

item.title: How long will the cold weather last?
item.description: Friday feels noticeably colder. Helen Willetts explains why there's been a change in temperature and looks at how long it may last.

item.title: Oil price profiteering will not be tolerated, says Miliband
item.description: Ed Miliband says the competition watchdog is primed to intervene if firms use the oil price shock to "rip off" customers.

item.title: UK troops at Iraq base shot down Iranian drones, Healey says
item.description: British forces based in Iraq shot down two drones overnight, Defence Secretary John Healey has confirmed.

item.title: US INTERVENTION IN OIL FUTURES WOULD BE ‘BIBLICAL DISASTER’, CME WARNS

item.title:
IRAN’S MISSILE LAUNCHER ARSENAL HOLDS STEADY DESPITE STRIKES
Despite heavy U.S. and Israeli strikes, about 60% of Iran’s missile launchers have been destroyed—little change from last week—showing how difficult it is to locate the small, mobile systems.
While attacks have dropped sharply and much of Iran’s air defenses are damaged, Tehran is likely preserving launchers and adjusting tactics, continuing to fire drones and missiles while keeping its remaining launch capacity intact.
item.description:
IRAN’S MISSILE LAUNCHER ARSENAL HOLDS STEADY DESPITE STRIKES

Despite heavy U.S. and Israeli strikes, about 60% of Iran’s missile launchers have been destroyed—little change from last week—showing how difficult it is to locate the small, mobile systems.

While attacks have dropped sharply and much of Iran’s air defenses are damaged, Tehran is likely preserving launchers and adjusting tactics, continuing to fire drones and missiles while keeping its remaining launch capacity intact.

item.title: S&P WEIGHS RULE CHANGES THAT WOULD SPEED SPACEX S&P 500 ENTRY
item.description: S&P WEIGHS RULE CHANGES THAT WOULD SPEED SPACEX S&P 500 ENTRY
